title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

description: Experience the true taste of Liguria with this authentic Focaccia Genovese, perfectly golden and aromatic. Infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil, its characteristic dimples hold pockets of savory goodness. This traditional Italian bread is a delightful accompaniment to any meal or a satisfying snack on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, granulated sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in 40ml of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms and all flour is incorporated.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. If using a stand mixer, knead with a dough hook for 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: First Proof
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean large bowl with a tablespoon of olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Dimple
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with remaining 30ml of olive oil. Gently transfer the dough to the pan and carefully stretch it to fill the pan. Use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Top
instructions.text: Drizzle a little more olive oil over the dimpled focaccia. Sprinkle with the chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 200C (400F). Bake the focaccia for 30-35 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The crust should be crisp and the inside soft.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven. If desired, drizzle with a final touch of extra virgin olive oil for extra flavor and sheen. Let it cool on a wire rack for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

tips: For the signature Genovese focaccia texture, ensure you create deep dimples in the dough before the second rise; these pockets will hold delicious olive oil.
Always use good quality extra virgin olive oil; it is a key flavor component. Fresh rosemary is also essential for the authentic aroma.
Focaccia is best enjoyed warm on the day it is baked. If you have leftovers, refresh it in a warm oven for a few minutes before serving.
article:
## Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil: A Taste of Ligurian Tradition

Imagine the aroma of freshly baked bread, kissed by fragrant rosemary and the sun-drenched richness of extra virgin olive oil. This isn't just any bread; it's Focaccia Genovese Rosemary, a culinary masterpiece hailing from the Liguria region of Italy. More than just a simple flatbread, this iconic bake is a symbol of Italian hospitality, a testament to simple, high-quality ingredients, and a truly unforgettable sensory experience. Known for its distinct dimpled surface, delicate crumb, and incredibly flavorful crust, Focaccia Genovese is a staple in Italian bakeries and homes, perfect for dipping, sharing, or simply savoring on its own. If you're ready to bring a piece of authentic Italian baking into your kitchen, join us on a journey to master this delightful creation.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

Focaccia Genovese, or "fugassa" in the local Ligurian dialect, is a traditional flatbread with roots deep in Genoa, the capital of Liguria. Unlike other focaccia varieties that might be thicker or have a cake-like crumb, the Genovese version is characterized by its relatively thin profile, often less than an inch thick, and its signature "dimples." These depressions are not just for aesthetics; they serve a crucial purpose. Before baking, a brine made of water and olive oil is poured over the surface, settling into these dimples, creating pockets of intense flavor and a wonderfully crisp, salty crust that contrasts beautifully with the soft interior. The liberal use of high-quality extra virgin olive oil is non-negotiable, contributing to its rich flavor, golden color, and unique texture. It’s a bread meant to be enjoyed fresh, often as a breakfast treat, a snack, or an accompaniment to meals, its savory goodness making it incredibly versatile.

## Ingredients for Rosemary Focaccia Genovese

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary requires attention to quality ingredients. Simplicity is key, so each component plays a vital role in achieving that authentic Ligurian taste and texture.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (Type 00 or All-Purpose with high protein):** About 500g. A higher protein content helps develop gluten, giving the focaccia its characteristic chewiness.
*   **Warm Water:** Around 350-400ml (approximately 1 ½ to 1 ¾ cups). The exact amount can vary based on your flour, so add gradually. It should be lukewarm, not hot, to activate the yeast without killing it.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (one packet or about 2 ¼ teaspoons). This is the leavening agent that makes the focaccia light and airy.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g (about 1 ½ teaspoons) for the dough, plus more for the brine and sprinkling. Essential for flavor balance.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 100ml (about ½ cup) for the dough, plus an additional 50-70ml for coating the pan, brushing, and the brine. Use a good quality, fruity olive oil; it makes a significant difference.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** A few sprigs, leaves stripped and roughly chopped. This is the star herb for our Focaccia Genovese Rosemary, providing its signature fragrant notes.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt:** For sprinkling on top before baking, adding a delightful crunch and burst of salinity.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Focaccia Genovese

Baking Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is a rewarding process that requires patience but yields incredible results.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and salt to the yeast mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Then, pour in 50ml of the extra virgin olive oil.
3.  **Knead the Dough:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 10-15 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook for about 8-10 minutes. The dough should be soft and pliable.
4.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Lightly grease a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turn it once to coat, and cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth. Let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Liberally grease a standard baking sheet (approx. 13x18 inches or 33x45 cm) with a generous amount of olive oil – don't be shy! This creates the crispy bottom crust.
6.  **Shape the Dough:** Gently deflate the risen dough and transfer it to the prepared baking sheet. Slowly stretch and press the dough to fill the pan. It might resist at first; if so, cover it and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before continuing to stretch. This is where the magic of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary truly begins.
7.  **Second Rise:** Once the dough fills the pan, cover it again and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place. It should look puffy and slightly increased in volume.
8.  **Dimple and Brine:** Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F). With oiled fingertips, gently but firmly press deep dimples all over the surface of the dough, making sure some reach the bottom of the pan. In a small bowl, whisk together 3 tablespoons of olive oil with 3 tablespoons of water and a pinch of fine sea salt to create a brine. Pour this mixture evenly over the dimpled focaccia, ensuring it pools in the depressions.
9.  **Add Rosemary and Salt:** Sprinkle the chopped fresh rosemary leaves evenly over the top, followed by a generous shower of flaky sea salt.
10. **Bake:** Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and deeply golden on the bottom.
11. **Finish:** As soon as it comes out of the oven, drizzle a little more extra virgin olive oil over the hot Focaccia Genovese Rosemary for extra flavor and shine. Let it cool slightly on a wire rack before slicing and serving.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that authentic Ligurian taste and texture for your Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is all about a few key details:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is Paramount:** This cannot be stressed enough. Use the best extra virgin olive oil you can afford. Its flavor profile is central to the focaccia's character. Don't skimp on the oil in the pan either; it's what creates that coveted crispy, golden base.
*   **Hydration is Key:** Focaccia dough is typically high in hydration, making it soft and slightly sticky. Don't be tempted to add too much extra flour during kneading. A sticky dough makes for a tender crumb.
*   **Patience with Rising:** Allow the dough ample time for both rises. Proper fermentation develops flavor and creates the airy texture. Don't rush it.
*   **Deep Dimples:** When dimpling, don't be shy! Press your fingers down firmly, almost to the bottom of the pan. These dimples catch the brine and olive oil, resulting in those irresistible pockets of crispiness and saltiness.
*   **Hot Oven:** A hot oven ensures a quick rise and a beautifully browned crust without drying out the interior.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia

Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways:

*   **As is:** Warm from the oven, perhaps with an extra drizzle of good olive oil, it's a sublime snack or breakfast item.
*   **With Cheese and Cured Meats:** Serve alongside a selection of Italian cheeses (like fresh mozzarella, provolone, or pecorino) and thinly sliced cured meats (prosciutto, salami).
*   **Alongside Soups and Salads:** Its hearty yet light texture makes it perfect for dipping into minestrone, tomato soup, or accompanying a fresh green salad.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite sandwich ingredients – grilled vegetables, pesto, mozzarella, or mortadella work wonderfully.
*   **Dipping:** A bowl of high-quality extra virgin olive oil, perhaps infused with balsamic vinegar, is the perfect companion for tearing off pieces of this fragrant bread.

## Variations and Substitutions for Focaccia Genovese

While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is a benchmark of perfection, there's always room for personal touches and experimentation:

*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional for this specific focaccia, you can experiment with other fresh herbs like thyme, oregano, or sage for different aromatic profiles.
*   **Toppings:** Beyond rosemary and flaky salt, consider adding other traditional Ligurian toppings. Cherry tomatoes (halved and pressed into the dimples), thinly sliced red onions, or black olives are popular choices.
*   **Garlic:** A few thinly sliced garlic cloves can be added along with the rosemary for an extra pungent kick.
*   **Flour Blends:** For a slightly nuttier flavor and denser texture, you can substitute a portion of the bread flour with whole wheat flour (e.g., 80% bread flour, 20% whole wheat).
*   **Sweet Focaccia:** Though less common for the Genovese style, some regions make sweet focaccia with sugar in the dough and toppings like grapes or figs.

Baking Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is more than just making bread; it's an experience that connects you to the rich culinary heritage of Italy. From the simple act of kneading the dough to the intoxicating aroma filling your kitchen and the first bite of its salty, oily, rosemary-infused goodness, it's a journey worth taking. Buon appetito!
